description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, cylindrical handle and ornate, symmetrical blades. The blades are wide, crescent-shaped, and feature intricate gold filigree patterns, giving the axe a decorative and possibly ceremonial appearance. The handle is dark, likely made of wood or metal, and is reinforced with several metallic bands and rings, adding both structural integrity and visual interest. The top and bottom of the handle are capped with metallic elements, and the central grip area appears to be smooth and straight, suitable for two-handed use. The axe head is securely mounted, with the blades extending equally on both sides, and the overall construction suggests durability and weight. The color palette is dominated by dark metallic tones with gold accents, contributing to a striking and formidable look.
description: A double-headed, ornate battle axe with gold filigree blades and a reinforced dark handle, featuring metallic bands and decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, crescent-shaped blades with gold filigree and the full handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin blade profile and cylindrical handle, with metallic bands visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the symmetrical double blades and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle structure.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle, likely wood or metal, reinforced with metallic bands and rings, smooth grip, no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, wide crescent-shaped blades with symmetrical edges, ornate gold filigree, no beard, and a central mounting hub.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible metallic reinforcement.
